req.title: Read patient (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-077 requires: (1) a clinician assigned to a patient receives the resource and triggers an AuditEvent, (2) a clinician NOT assigned is denied with HTTP 403 and an AuditEvent is still emitted, (3) boundary conditions around assignment edges (e.g., a patient ID that exists but the clinician was just removed from the care team). EP partitions the assigned/unassigned clinician space; NEG covers the forbidden access path; BVA targets the edge where assignment is revoked immediately before the read; STC models the state change of assignment -> revoked -> read; DT captures all combinations of assignment x patient-exists.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Clinician is assigned to the patient's care team
actions: Obtain a valid bearer token for the assigned clinician via <get_auth_token>
Poll AuditEvent endpoint for an event referencing this patient read
expected: At least one AuditEvent exists with outcome '0' (success) referencing the patient id and clinician id
trace: R-HLT-077: resource returned for assigned clinician
R-HLT-077: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    fhir_base_url, assigned_clinician_credentials, assigned_patient_id, audit_poller
):
    token = <get_auth_token>(assigned_clinician_credentials)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/fhir+json"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == assigned_patient_id

    audit = audit_poller(
        criteria={
            "entity": assigned_patient_id,
            "agent": assigned_clinician_credentials["user_id"],
            "outcome": "0"
        }
    )
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent with outcome=0 not found"
    assert audit["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403_and_audit
preconditions: Clinician is NOT assigned to the patient's care team
actions: Obtain a valid bearer token for the unassigned clinician via <get_auth_token>
Poll AuditEvent endpoint for a denial event referencing this attempt
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue severity 'error'
At least one AuditEvent exists with outcome '8' (serious failure/denied) referencing the patient id and clinician id
trace: R-HLT-077: AuditEvent emitted for every operation including denials
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403_and_audit(
    fhir_base_url, unassigned_clinician_credentials, unassigned_patient_id, audit_poller
):
    token = <get_auth_token>(unassigned_clinician_credentials)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/fhir+json"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)

    audit = audit_poller(
        criteria={
            "entity": unassigned_patient_id,
            "agent": unassigned_clinician_credentials["user_id"],
            "outcome": "8"
        }
    )
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent with outcome=8 (denied) not found"
    assert audit["outcome"] == "8"

tests.name: test_clinician_reads_patient_immediately_after_care_team_removal_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician WAS assigned to the patient's care team
Admin removes the clinician from the care team immediately before the read
actions: Obtain a valid bearer token for the now-removed clinician via <get_auth_token>
Admin call removes clinician from care team via <remove_care_team_member>
HTTP GET /Patient/{boundary_patient_id} with the clinician's token immediately after removal
Poll AuditEvent endpoint for a denial event referencing this attempt
expected: AuditEvent emitted with outcome '8' reflecting the post-removal denial
trace: R-HLT-077: 403 when clinician not assigned (boundary: assignment just revoked)
R-HLT-077: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
BVA: boundary between assigned and unassigned states
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_clinician_reads_patient_immediately_after_care_team_removal_returns_403(
    fhir_base_url, boundary_clinician_credentials, boundary_patient_id,
    admin_credentials, audit_poller
):
    admin_token = <get_auth_token>(admin_credentials)
    <remove_care_team_member>(
        fhir_base_url,
        admin_token,
        patient_id=boundary_patient_id,
        clinician_id=boundary_clinician_credentials["user_id"]
    )

    token = <get_auth_token>(boundary_clinician_credentials)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/fhir+json"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{boundary_patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"

    audit = audit_poller(
        criteria={
            "entity": boundary_patient_id,
            "agent": boundary_clinician_credentials["user_id"],
            "outcome": "8"
        }
    )
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent for post-removal denial not found"

tests.name: test_decision_table_assigned_and_unassigned_clinicians_audit_outcomes
preconditions: Two clinician accounts exist: one assigned, one unassigned
A single patient resource exists in fhir-lite
actions: For each (clinician, expected_http_status, expected_audit_outcome) row in decision table:
expected: All four decision table rows produce correct HTTP and audit outcomes
trace: R-HLT-077: resource returned for assigned clinician
R-HLT-077: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
DT: cartesian product of assignment x patient-exists conditions

tests.name: test_state_transition_assigned_read_revoke_read_yields_403
preconditions: Clinician is initially assigned to the patient's care team
actions: State S1 (assigned): GET /Patient/{id} -> expect 200 + AuditEvent outcome=0
Transition: Admin revokes care team assignment via <remove_care_team_member>
State S2 (unassigned): GET /Patient/{id} -> expect 403 + AuditEvent outcome=8
expected: S1 read returns HTTP 200 with correct Patient resource
AuditEvent outcome='0' exists for S1 read
S2 read returns HTTP 403 after revocation
AuditEvent outcome='8' exists for S2 read
trace: R-HLT-077: resource returned for assigned clinician (S1)
R-HLT-077: AuditEvent emitted for every operation in both states
STC: assigned -> revoked state transition covered
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_state_transition_assigned_read_revoke_read_yields_403(
    fhir_base_url, stc_clinician_credentials, stc_patient_id, admin_credentials, audit_poller
):
    token = <get_auth_token>(stc_clinician_credentials)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/fhir+json"}
    agent_id = stc_clinician_credentials["user_id"]

    resp_s1 = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{stc_patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_s1.status_code == 200
    audit_s1 = audit_poller(criteria={"entity": stc_patient_id, "agent": agent_id, "outcome": "0"})
    assert audit_s1 is not None, "S1 AuditEvent outcome=0 not found"

    admin_token = <get_auth_token>(admin_credentials)
    <remove_care_team_member>(fhir_base_url, admin_token, stc_patient_id, agent_id)

    resp_s2 = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{stc_patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_s2.status_code == 403
    audit_s2 = audit_poller(criteria={"entity": stc_patient_id, "agent": agent_id, "outcome": "8"})
    assert audit_s2 is not None, "S2 AuditEvent outcome=8 not found"
